deep_dive.title: When Is a Draft Accepted? A Theory of Acceptance in Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates language model inference by using a fast drafter to propose candidate tokens that are then verified by a larger target model. Existing theory largely studies the stochastic, distribution-preserving setting, where the goal is to exactly sample from the target distribution. In contrast, many practical systems use greedy decoding, relaxed acceptance rules, or tree-based candidate sets, where success is governed by local ranking and threshold events rather than exact distributional equality. We develop a theory for these regimes. We identify that many common acceptance criteria have rejection regions that can be characterized as lower level sets of the target distribution. For these, we characterize the exact KL divergence required for rejection yielding exact certificates and sharp margin-based bounds for strict greedy decoding, additive and multiplicative relaxed acceptance, top-(m) relaxed criteria, and entropy-thresholded acceptance. We then extend the framework to greedy tree decoding, deriving exact and margin-only certificates for when the target greedy token remains covered by the drafter's top-(m) candidates. Finally, we evaluate the resulting certificates on Qwen3 models, showing that relaxed and tree-based criteria substantially enlarge the region of certified acceptance, especially on decoding steps with low target model distribution margin. These results complement existing distribution-preserving analyses of speculative decoding by characterizing the deterministic local acceptance events common in practical inference systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: theory of acceptance in speculative decoding with relaxed verification and greedy decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 投机解码中接受理论，涵盖宽松验证和贪婪解码设置
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 投机解码中接受理论，涵盖宽松验证和贪婪解码设置
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 投机解码中接受理论，涵盖宽松验证和贪婪解码设置
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper develops a theory for speculative decoding with relaxed acceptance rules, greedy decoding, and tree-based candidates, characterizing rejection regions and KL divergence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有投机解码理论多针对精确采样场景，而实际系统常采用贪婪解码、宽松接受规则或树状候选集。本文为这些场景建立统一理论，将常见接受准则的拒绝域表征为目标分布的低水平集，并推导精确KL散度。该理论为设计高效实用的投机解码验证策略提供了理论基础，直接支持宽松验证机制下的推理加速。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有投机解码理论多针对精确采样场景，而实际系统常采用贪婪解码、宽松接受规则或树状候选集。本文为这些场景建立统一理论，将常见接受准则的拒绝域表征为目标分布的低水平集，并推导精确KL散度。该理论为设计高效实用的投机解码验证策略提供了理论基础，直接支持宽松验证机制下的推理加速。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: SeKV: Resolution-Adaptive KV Cache with Hierarchical Semantic Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models increasingly operate over long contexts, where the KV cache becomes a dominant memory bottleneck: its size grows linearly with sequence length and must be retained throughout decoding, making full GPU caching prohibitively expensive without compression. Existing KV cache compression methods struggle to balance efficiency with faithful context preservation. Token eviction discards information, while semantic grouping fixes compression decisions at prefill time; neither can recover token-level detail from a compressed span once it becomes relevant during generation. As a solution, we propose SeKV, a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that organizes context into entropy-guided semantic spans and stores them across a GPU-CPU memory hierarchy without discarding information. Each span keeps a lightweight summary vector on GPU for coarse routing and a low-rank SVD basis on CPU for on-demand token-level reconstruction. A trained zoom-in mechanism selectively expands query-relevant spans during decoding, enabling precise retrieval without materializing the full KV cache on GPU. SeKV enables adaptive token-level reconstruction while keeping the base LLM fully frozen and adding fewer than 0.05% trainable parameters. Across four benchmarks, SeKV improves over the strongest semantic compression baseline by 5.9% on average while reducing GPU memory by 53.3% versus full KV caching at 128K context. Code is available on https://github.com/AmirAbaskohi/SeKV.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向高效LLM推理的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向高效LLM推理的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向高效LLM推理的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SeKV, a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that organizes context into entropy-guided semantic spans with multiple resolutions, enabling recovery of token-level details and balancing efficiency with context preservation for long-context LLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型长上下文推理中KV缓存成为瓶颈。本文提出SeKV，一种分辨率自适应语义KV缓存，将上下文组织成熵引导的语义跨度并以多分辨率存储，在压缩的同时保留恢复令牌级细节的能力。实验表明SeKV在保持推理质量的同时显著降低缓存内存占用，为长上下文LLM高效推理提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型长上下文推理中KV缓存成为瓶颈。本文提出SeKV，一种分辨率自适应语义KV缓存，将上下文组织成熵引导的语义跨度并以多分辨率存储，在压缩的同时保留恢复令牌级细节的能力。实验表明SeKV在保持推理质量的同时显著降低缓存内存占用，为长上下文LLM高效推理提供了新范式。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

quick_skim.title: Depth Exploration for LLM Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive LLM decoding evaluates every generated token through the full layer stack, even though many tokens become predictable at intermediate depths. Existing lossless depth-adaptive methods exploit this redundancy by choosing a single non-final exit depth and verifying its prediction with the final-depth model. However, our measurements show that this selection-based strategy leaves substantial headroom: choosing an exit too late wastes computation, while choosing one too early triggers fallback and discards dependent drafts. We propose Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX), a lossless decoding algorithm that replaces single-depth selection with parallel exploration over multiple candidate depths. At each commit position, DEX validates candidates against the final-depth reference, commits exactly the final-depth token, and collapses the exploration lattice to retain only reusable branch states. This expand--commit--collapse procedure preserves equivalence to standard autoregressive decoding while reducing the cost of committing each token. Across early-exit-trained and standard LLMs, DEX outperforms representative depth-selection baselines and achieves competitive end-to-end throughput against speculative and distributed decoding methods. Moreover, DEX improves as the explored depths become finer, showing that parallel depth exploration provides a scalable way to exploit the underused depth axis of LLM decoding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: depth exploration decoding with parallel candidate validation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度探索解码与并行候选验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度探索解码与并行候选验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度探索解码与并行候选验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX) that validates multiple candidate depths in parallel against the final-layer reference, achieving lossless acceleration for LLM decoding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有深度自适应解码只能选择一个退出点，过早或过晚都会浪费计算或触发回退。本文提出深度探索解码，同时探索多个候选深度，并行验证后选择最佳结果，在保持无损的前提下进一步加速LLM自回归解码。该方法通过平行验证避免了单点选择的权衡，显著提升效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有深度自适应解码只能选择一个退出点，过早或过晚都会浪费计算或触发回退。本文提出深度探索解码，同时探索多个候选深度，并行验证后选择最佳结果，在保持无损的前提下进一步加速LLM自回归解码。该方法通过平行验证避免了单点选择的权衡，显著提升效率。

quick_skim.title: Speculative Pre-Positioning: Decoding Stateful Sessions to the Next Decision Point Off the Critical Path
quick_skim.abstract: A stateless inference server (vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM) idles between requests while the accelerator waits; a stateful session reclaims that idle time. Speculative pre-positioning decodes the session forward to its next decision point with the target model's own forward pass and no draft model, moving the cross-request prefill and entry-decode off the critical path: the next request resumes from a pre-paid entry on its delta, or, when a confidence gate fires, is answered from a cached distribution in one near-constant vocabulary scan with no decode, at a cost only of energy and a rare, bounded false accept. The payoff is conditional on capability: a capable model fires the gate at near-full coverage and about 87% precision (a smaller one never clears it), returning the first token in about 1.0 ms versus the 39 ms decode a prefix cache still pays.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speculative pre-positioning with confidence gate for LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 投机预定位与置信门控的LLM推理加速
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 投机预定位与置信门控的LLM推理加速
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 投机预定位与置信门控的LLM推理加速
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces speculative pre-positioning that decodes stateful LLM sessions off the critical path using target model forward passes and a confidence gate for near-zero-latency responses.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出投机预定位方法，利用目标模型自身的前向传播在非关键路径上预解码会话，并通过置信门控实现接近零延迟的服务响应。该方法无需草稿模型，在保持高质量的同时大幅降低响应延迟，适用于状态ful推理服务器。

quick_skim.title: Predict, Reuse, and Repair: Accelerating Dynamic Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Dynamic sparse attention (DSA) accelerates long-context LLM decoding by attending to only the top-K KV blocks relevant to each query, but it introduces a serialized selection-to-attention dependency that emerges as a new latency bottleneck. We present PRR, a speculate-reuse-repair runtime that exploits temporal locality in DSA selections to predict likely blocks, speculate the attention over them while selection is in flight, and incrementally repair missed blocks once the true selected set is known. PRR uses a lightweight EMA-based predictor, a profiling-guided speculation budget that keeps speculative work off the critical path, and a FlashAttention-based repair kernel that folds missed blocks into the partial attention state using online-softmax statistics. Across long-context benchmarks and representative DSA methods, PRR reduces per-token decoding latency by up to 40% while preserving downstream task accuracy. Github: https://github.com/Tianyu9748/Incremental_FlashAttention
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speculate-reuse-repair runtime for accelerating LLM decoding via dynamic sparse attention
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PRR accelerates long-context LLM decoding by predicting likely KV blocks, speculatively attending them, and repairing missed ones, exploiting temporal locality in dynamic sparse attention.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 动态稀疏注意力虽加速长上下文LLM解码，却引入串行选择-注意依赖导致新瓶颈。本文提出PRR运行时，利用选择的时间局部性预测候选KV块，并发投机注意，再增量修复遗漏块。该方法结合EMA预测器、分析引导的投机预算和基于FlashAttention的修复核，在不改变注意机制的前提下显著降低解码延迟。实验表明PRR有效消除选择瓶颈，对长上下文场景的推理加速具有重要实用价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 动态稀疏注意力虽加速长上下文LLM解码，却引入串行选择-注意依赖导致新瓶颈。本文提出PRR运行时，利用选择的时间局部性预测候选KV块，并发投机注意，再增量修复遗漏块。该方法结合EMA预测器、分析引导的投机预算和基于FlashAttention的修复核，在不改变注意机制的前提下显著降低解码延迟。实验表明PRR有效消除选择瓶颈，对长上下文场景的推理加速具有重要实用价值。
